doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_mpz_init2 (mpfr/src/pool.c L55-L80). In C, the function either reclaims an mpz_t from the static mpz_tab pool (if n_alloc > 0 AND n fits within MPFR_POOL_MAX_SIZE * GMP_NUMB_BITS = 32 * 64 = 2048 bits) or calls the real mpz_init2 to pre-allocate at least n bits of limb storage; in both cases the SIZ is set to 0 so the value is +0.

The TS port is a vacuous factory taking a bit-count hint n (ignored) and returning 0n. Per ADR 0003 (bigint-as-mpz), there is no allocator, no pool, no explicit init: a bigint is JS-runtime managed. The n parameter is accepted to match the C contract surface but discarded -- JS bigints have no pre-allocation knob, growth is amortised by the runtime.

The count-passthrough-with-hint shape mirrors mpfr_inits2 (which takes a count and returns it) but in this case the hint is the bit-count, not the function-call count.

Tag minimums per Rule 7: happy 20, edge 30, adversarial 12, fuzz 50, mined 5.
divergence_from_c:
Four divergences:

  1. Return: C void (mutates z in place) -> TS returns 0n (the value of a freshly-initialised bigint mpz analogue).

  2. Parameter: C mp_bitcnt_t n is a pre-allocation HINT (the C pre-allocates >= n bits of limb storage to avoid realloc churn); TS accepts a bigint n >= 0 but DISCARDS it (JS bigint allocator has no API for pre-sizing).

  3. Pool: C reclaims from a static thread-local pool when n fits in the pool's per-entry cap (2048 bits); TS has no pool. Functional output -- a +0 mpz -- is identical regardless of which C branch fires.

  4. Domain: C signature is mp_bitcnt_t (unsigned long, [0, 2^64-1]); TS accepts bigint n >= 0n. Negative n throws MPFRError('EDOMAIN'); the upper bound is implicit (JS bigint has no max).
refs: mpfr/src/pool.c L55-L80 -- the C reference body.
eval/functions/mpfr_mpz_init/spec.json -- the no-arg sister (init without size hint).
eval/functions/mpfr_inits2/spec.json -- precedent (count+prec passthrough for vacuous init family).
docs/adr/0003-mpz-api.md -- bigint-as-mpz_t; no pool, no allocator.
